hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric quantifies the degree to which a neural network potential's learned energy landscape violates physical self-consistency, then incorporating the Force Delta as an explicit regularization term during fine-tuning of pre-trained foundation models on domain-specific data will simultaneously reduce the Force Delta magnitude and improve generalization to out-of-distribution configurations more effectively than standard force-matching loss alone, because penalizing non-conservative behavior during training directly constrains the model toward physically consistent potential energy surfaces rather than merely fitting observed force vectors.
